name: La Mola, Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt – l'Obac and Sant Llorenç del Munt – Circular Route
name_original: La Mola, Monasterio de Sant Llorenç del Munt – l'Obac y Sant Llorenç del Munt – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking around Matadepera offers access to the diverse and rugged landscapes of the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac. This region is characterized by imposing rock formations, cliffs, and monoliths of red conglomerates, sculpted by erosion. The area features two main mountain ranges, Sant Llorenç del Munt and L'Obac, with peaks like La Mola (1,104 meters) and Montcau (1,057 meters) providing extensive views. The terrain includes pine forests, holm oaks, and Mediterranean shrubs, creating varied natural environments for exploration. Best hiking trails around Matadepera The most popular hiking route is Path of the Monks to La Mola, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate path leads to the historic Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt at the summit of La Mola. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Mola, Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt – l'Obac and Sant Llorenç del Munt – Circular Route, a difficult 6.1 miles (9.9 km) path. This route explores the prominent peaks and the monastery, offering panoramic views across the park. Local hikers also love the Holes of Can Pobla – La Mola (1,103 m) loop from Sant Llorenç del Munt, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail leading through unique rock formations, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Matadepera is defined by striking rock formations, dense pine and holm oak forests, and significant peaks like La Mola. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Matadepera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 7,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Matadepera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Matadepera?
A: Matadepera offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 90 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, including easy, moderate, and challenging options, ensuring there's something for everyone to explore in the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families in Matadepera?
A: Yes, Matadepera has several easy trails perfect for families. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed here, the region offers paths that are less strenuous and allow for enjoyable walks amidst nature. Look for routes marked as 'easy' on komoot for suitable options.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes near Matadepera?
A: The hiking trails around Matadepera are renowned for their striking natural features. You'll encounter unique red conglomerate rock formations, dramatic cliffs, and dense forests of pine and holm oak. The area is part of the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac, offering diverse landscapes and panoramic views from peaks like La Mola and Montcau.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Matadepera?
A: Yes, Matadepera offers several circular hiking routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952586260/la-mola-monastery-of-sant-llorenc-del-munt-l-obac-and-sant-llorenc-del-munt-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola, Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt – l'Obac and Sant Llorenç del Munt – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 9.9 km path exploring prominent peaks and the historic monastery. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38208098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Holes of Can Pobla – La Mola (1,103 m) loop from Sant Llorenç del Munt&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.1 km trail through unique rock formations.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest to visit while hiking in Matadepera?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Matadepera's trails lead to several captivating landmarks. The Romanesque Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt, located at the summit of La Mola, is a significant historical attraction. You can reach it via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939046351/path-of-the-monks-to-la-mola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of the Monks to La Mola&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the region features unique geological formations like the Morro del Drac, which can be explored on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1474140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola Summit – Morral del Drac loop from el Salt&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any gorges or water features to discover near Matadepera's hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area around Matadepera is home to several intriguing gorges and water features. You can explore places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2701406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal dels Micos&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2999385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pools of the Font dels Llaminers&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg d'en Fitó&lt;/a&gt;. These spots offer a refreshing contrast to the rugged mountain terrain and are great for exploring natural water formations.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Matadepera?
A: The hiking trails in Matadepera are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning panoramic views from peaks like La Mola, the unique rock formations, and the well-maintained paths that lead through diverse natural environments within the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to viewpoints with extensive vistas?
A: Absolutely. The peaks within the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac, particularly La Mola (1,104 meters) and Montcau (1,057 meters), offer spectacular panoramic views. On clear days, these vistas can extend from the Pyrenees to the Garraf mountain range, and even reach Mallorca. Many routes, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952586260/la-mola-monastery-of-sant-llorenc-del-munt-l-obac-and-sant-llorenc-del-munt-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola, Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt – l'Obac and Sant Llorenç del Munt – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, are designed to showcase these breathtaking viewpoints.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through unique forest types, like beech forests?
A: While the dominant forests are pine and holm oak, the region's diverse flora includes hazelnut groves in shadier valleys. Some sections of longer routes, such as parts of the extensive 'Els 3 Monts' route that traverses the park, are known to lead through beautiful beech forests, offering a different kind of natural experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Matadepera?
A: Matadepera is an excellent hiking destination year-round, but the best time often depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, making them ideal for longer hikes. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter hiking is also possible, with cooler temperatures and potentially clearer views, though some higher elevation paths might require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural complexes near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, beyond the Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt, the region boasts the historical-cultural complex of Puig de la Balma. This site features buildings dating back to the 12th century, built directly into the rock, offering a unique glimpse into Catalan rural life and history. It's a fascinating stop for those interested in the area's heritage.
tips: La Mola can be reached by several paths. The most typical are the Camino de los Monjes and Can Robert, where there is a car park nearby. Can Robert is a traditional farmhouse with two floors. On the main façade it has a voussoired portal and straight lintel windows, one of them decorated. Next to the farmhouse there are remains of an oil mill, a lime oven, tanks and other farm elements. The farmhouse has been documented with this name since the 18th century, but it is probable that there was an earlier settlement, from medieval times.

Avenc Terrós is a small cave 7 meters deep. It has two entrances.

Avenc del Figuerot is a very narrow chasm 55 meters deep.

They should only be explored with a caving club.

Can Pobla is a farmhouse in Matadepera. It was built in 1905. It is protected as a Cultural Property of Local Interest. Of the group of buildings that make up Can Pobla, the summer house of the Quadras family stands out.

Cueva de Manel is quite popular; You don't need rope or any specific material, but it's not easy. The narrowness of its galleries makes it not suitable for many people. Entering the cave is recommended only if you are with an experienced group.

The continuous action of the erosive agents on some rocks and cliffs of the massif has caused small holes. They do not have the depth of hollow caves, but they are also often attractively shaped and offer good views of the landscape. Another phenomenon, with very similar results, is generated by needles resting on a cliff and leaving a large hole in the middle. These types of arcades are usually called rock bridges.

The Cova de L'Hort dels Monjos and the three cavities on the right hand side are located on the eastern slope of Roca Colom, in an area known as Horts dels Monjos, where there is an abundance of soil rich in substrate and where the community of monks from the old monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt cultivated.

The highest peak in the natural park. Here is the Romanesque monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt (10th century). There is also a very popular restaurant where you can enjoy one of the most spectacular views over much of Catalonia, thanks to the 1,104 m height.

Fantastic place to visit with family, although on weekends it gets very crowded. It is one of the few examples of Catalan Romanesque that preserves its original plan. The bell tower is low and it is speculated that it was not finished. Next to the temple there is a wing, possibly a section of the cloister where a restaurant is located today. An interesting fact is that everything that the restaurant needs for its operation is carried to the top of the mountain on mules.

The Canal dels Micos is one of the classic canals of Montserrat. The first part has to advance climbing like monkeys (Monkeys) thanks to the roots of the trees. The final objective is spectacular, the Miranda dels Ecos, a privileged vantage point in the northern area of Montserrat. Note: the trail is probably not suitable for inexperienced hikers.
Take a moment to appreciate the spectacular views from here.

It is located on the northern slope of La Mola (1,103.3 m), the highest point of the massif, at a distance of about 550 meters from the summit. It is in the pass between the summit of La Mola and the hill of Griera (1,002.4), at the beginning of the Pagès ridge, which forms the backbone of the massif of Sant Llorenç del Munt up to the pass of Eres, southeast of Montcau. It has an elevation of 980.2 m. It is in the municipality of Matadepera, in Vallès Occidental. On the south-west side of the pass where it is located there is the Coma de l'Abella canal and on the north-east side there is the Santa Agnès canal, where the chapel of Santa Agnès is located.
It is a monolith of conglomerate with a carbonate composition formed by two large blocks of about 32 meters high, separated at their base, where they leave a large opening.
It is the scene of one of the most remote traditional legends of Sant Llorenç del Munt, the legend of the dragon of Sant Llorenç. The first ascent was made on February 11, 1923 by members of the Terrassa Hiking Center.

The Sescorts caves, also called Les Corts and Los Estables, are located below the Morral del Drac, on the path that goes from the Estenalles pass to La Mola. The caves are close to the Coma de l'Abella cliff, at the foot of the Petanta Rock. They were used a few years ago to keep cattle and we can still find the roof quite smoky from the fire on the ground that they made. There are 7 small cavities with an approximate length of 25 meters. From inside there are views of the monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt, at the top of the summit of La Mola.
Channel path to join la Mola on NW direction.

The Mal Pas is an easy section equipped with a steel cable. Follow the green marks until you find the steel towline. The passage does not have any special complications, only in case of rain you have to be more careful.

The necropolis is located about 50m from the Can Robert farmhouse, protected by a wooden fence. There is an information panel with more information. There are 7 tombs in total, spaced equidistantly. The existence of the site has been known since 1923.
